issues: item palette skews warm/muted — missing saturated blues and pinks called out in brief
icon set feels slightly generic; lacks hand-pixeled personality compared to Denzi reference detail density
corner gem decorations are competent but the ornamental language could be rounder/friendlier
reasoning: Emotional axis (60%): Strong pass. The warm golden-brick border, cream interior, and ornate corner medallions land squarely in toy-diorama Hyrule territory. Item icons (tree, carrot, mushroom, jar, heart-gem, lightning bolt) are readable and charming with zero grim/scary energy. Color temperature is consistently warm. Loses a point because the overall palette stays in gold/tan/red range and almost entirely skips the saturated greens, blues, and pinks the brief requires — saturation feels dialled back rather than bright and popping. Emotional score ~7.5. Technical axis (40%): Passes. Hard 1px outlines are visible and consistent across border tiles and item silhouettes at all three zoom levels; no obvious anti-aliasing halos. Shading reads as 2-3 tone hard-step on the corner decorations and most icons. Pixel grid alignment looks correct throughout. Slight concern: the smallest item glyphs (shield slots, potion outlines) are very minimal and lose the hand-pixeled craft feel at native resolution. Technical score ~6.5. Weighted composite: (7.5×0.6)+(6.5×0.4) = 4.5+2.6 = 7.1 → 7. Verdict marginal; needs a pass of colour enrichment on the item icons and a touch more personality/detail in the hand-pixel work before it clears the 8-threshold.
